task_metadata.current_task: Implement automatic cleanup of expired timestamps during every allow_request check and periodic cleanup of idle clients
task_metadata.status: Memory management strategy defined, ready for core implementation

nodes.description: Choose sliding window algorithm for rate limiting implementation to accurately track requests per minute
nodes.rationale: Sliding window provides precise request tracking by maintaining a timestamp-based window of exactly one minute, ensuring accurate rate limiting that prevents request bunching at minute boundaries unlike fixed window approach
nodes.task_state: Initializing rate limiter design, algorithm selection in progress

nodes.description: Use in-memory dictionary with queue per client to store request timestamps for sliding window calculation
nodes.rationale: Dictionary keyed by client ID with deque for each client provides O(1) lookup and efficient timestamp insertion/removal. Deque supports O(1) popleft operations needed for removing expired timestamps from the window
nodes.task_state: Data structure selection complete, ready for implementation

nodes.description: In-memory storage for request timestamps will accumulate unbounded if stale client entries are not cleaned up, potentially causing memory exhaustion
nodes.rationale: Without periodic cleanup of inactive clients or expired timestamps, the dictionary will grow indefinitely as new clients make requests. Each timestamp is stored until it exits the sliding window, requiring active garbage collection

nodes.description: Implement automatic cleanup of expired timestamps during every allow_request check and periodic cleanup of idle clients
nodes.rationale: Proactive removal of timestamps older than 60 seconds during each request check prevents unbounded growth. Adding optional periodic cleanup job ensures even idle clients eventually get cleared from the dictionary
nodes.task_state: Memory management strategy defined, ready for core implementation

edges.rationale: The dictionary with deque data structure is the concrete implementation of the sliding window algorithm design decision
The cleanup strategy directly resolves the unbounded memory growth constraint by ensuring expired timestamps and idle clients are regularly removed
